behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A unit test of only the grouping function can miss real failures in field mapping and ingestion wiring across service-specific log shapes. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking parser/normalizer boundaries could hide trace-id extraction mismatches. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingest + parse + correlate modules together on mixed JSON logs from multiple services (different field order/extra fields), then assert one correlated request group per shared trace_id across services.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A pure sort unit test may miss failures from timestamp parsing/normalization plus timeline assembly. REAL-INFRA: No external OS/network dependency is inherently required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking time normalization can hide skew-window boundary bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Feed real parser + correlator + timeline builder with cross-service logs whose timestamps are intentionally offset within and just beyond 500ms; assert final timeline order and skew handling rules at boundaries.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit-testing percentile math alone can miss failures in how baseline and current windows are wired into anomaly reporting. REAL-INFRA: No real network/OS needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking metric sources can make threshold logic appear correct while report integration is broken. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Execute metrics aggregation + baseline computation + anomaly reporter together on synthetic latency series where p99 crosses 3x baseline (and near-threshold controls), then assert anomaly flags and report payload.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit tests of parser exceptions can miss pipeline-level crash handling and skip-counter propagation. REAL-INFRA: No external system required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking parser errors may hide real malformed-input edge cases and counter/report wiring bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingestion pipeline on a batch mixing valid and malformed JSON lines; assert process completes, malformed records are skipped, and reported skipped-count exactly matches malformed inputs.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: In-process mocks cannot reliably expose scheduler, buffering, I/O, and backpressure behavior under load. REAL-INFRA: Requires real runtime/process and OS resource behavior to catch throughput/latency collapse. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked queues/storage can falsely pass while real pipeline drops events. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end load test against the built service/process using a real high-rate log producer at 10k entries/sec, measure ingest lag and output counts over sustained burst, and assert zero drops plus bounded backlog.
